data.title: AtkinsRealis Group Price Target Raised to $92 at RBC
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets raised its price target on AtkinsRealis Group Inc. to $92 from $89 on Friday.

Analyst Sabahat Khan maintained an Outperform rating on shares of the Montreal-based engineering and construction company following its quarterly results.

"AtkinsRealis reported consolidated Q4 results that were short of expectations (good results from Engineering + Nuclear more than offset by a larger-than-expected LSTK loss), while 2025 guidance was in line with expectations and reflects another year of solid top-line growth and margin expansion in the 2 core segments," Khan said in a note to clients.

"The solid 2025 guide and a clean pro-forma balance sheet positions the company well for the year ahead," the analyst said.

data.title: Science Applications International Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.37 a Share, Payable April 25 to Holders as of April 11
Tensions Rise Over Apple's Encryption Policy as U.K. and U.S. Hold High-Level Talks
data.description:
U.K. and U.S. officials have held private talks to address concerns that Britain is pressuring Apple to create a back door into encrypted user data, Bloomberg News reported. British officials claim they are not seeking broad access but want to correct what they call a misleading narrative.

The discussions follow Apple's decision to disable end-to-end encryption for cloud data in the U.K., complying with a government order. The move has sparked backlash, with U.S. Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard calling it an egregious violation of American rights.

Apple is challenging the order, with a closed-door hearing set for Friday at London's High Court. U.K. officials insist data requests would be limited to serious crimes like terrorism and child exploitation, requiring separate warrants and primarily targeting U.K. individuals, not U.S. citizens.

Meanwhile, human rights groups Liberty and Privacy International have joined Apple in opposing the directive, calling it a threat to global security. A bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers has also pushed for the U.K. court to remove a gag order they argue stifles free speech and limits congressional oversight.

Despite the dispute, British officials stress that intelligence cooperation between the two countries remains strong and will continue under the new U.S. administration.

data.title: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Retains Quarterly Dividend at $0.25 a Share, Payable April 15 to Holders of Record as of March 31
Jampack Elevates Security Standards in Event Travel Packages, Now SOC 2 Type 1 Certified
data.description:
Certification Demonstrates Commitment to Data Security, Risk Management, and Operational Excellence in the Rapidly Growing Travel Technology Sector

Las Vegas, Nevada--(Newsfile Corp. - March 14, 2025) - Jampack, the leading platform in ticket and hotel packages for live events, has successfully obtained its Service Organization Control (SOC) 2 Type 1 certification. This achievement validates Jampack's adherence to industry standards, reinforcing its commitment to maintaining a secure and reliable infrastructure for its enterprise clients.

Jampack, the leading platform specializing in ticket and hotel packages

"Earning SOC 2 Type 1 certification demonstrates Jampack's continued dedication to maintaining rigorous security and operational excellence," said Andrew Citores, CEO of Jampack. "As the company connects event producers, tour operators, and integrated resorts with travelers, maintaining trust and protecting data are essential. This certification provides our partners and their customers with assurance that sensitive information is safeguarded at an enterprise-grade level."

Importance of SOC 2 Certification

Trust and security are non-negotiable for a platform servicing event travel bookings for hundreds of thousands of customers and safeguarding their personal information. The SOC 2 Type 1 certification represents an independent verification that Jampack has implemented appropriate controls to safeguard data within the platform.

For Jampack customers and partners, this certification signifies:

  • Validated security measures

  • Enhanced trust and reliability

  • Reduced counterparty risk for our partners

  • Enterprise-grade infrastructure

Strengthening the Travel Technology Ecosystem

The travel industry is increasingly relying on technology partners to create seamless booking experiences. When selecting these partners, companies naturally consider the security risks involved. The SOC 2 certification provides assurance that:

  • Jampack has designed appropriate security controls
  • Jampack's platform meets the needs of enterprise-level operations Customer data is protected throughout the booking journey

This certification is particularly valuable for companies who need to ensure they're working with reliable, secure technology providers as they expand their digital offerings.

About Jampack's Platform

Jampack is the leading platform for creating comprehensive travel packages, seamlessly bundling event tickets, hotel accommodations, exclusive experiences, and perks. Serving over 180,000 attendees across more than 400 events, Jampack empowers event producers, tour operators, and integrated resorts to increase revenue, simplify operations, and elevate guest experiences-all without the need to manage complex technology in-house.

To learn more about how Jampack can streamline your event offerings and boost attendee satisfaction, visit www.jampack.com.

SOC 2 Type 1 Badge

Press inquiries

Jampack

https://www.jampack.com

William Brooks

press@jampack.com

7165 Rafael Ridge Way, Las Vegas, NV 89119

data.title: Canada's Carney Is an Economic Heavyweight. That May Not Matter in a Trump Trade Fight. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Brian Swint

The next prime minister of Canada is in many ways the polar opposite of U.S. President Donald Trump. That may give him an advantage — or end up working against him.

Mark Carney is so many things Donald Trump isn't — academic, cosmopolitan, and from a middle-class background in the remote West of the country. After a career in finance and stints as governor of both the Bank of Canada and the Bank of England — the only person to ever lead two Group of Seven central banks — he is being sworn in as Justin Trudeau's replacement on Friday after winning the contest to become Liberal Party leader.

The question is whether he has what it takes to make Canada thrive as Trump tries to turn it into the 51st state. Carney likes to say trade wars are like ice hockey — Canada will win.

The incoming prime minister is "someone who ultimately cares about people, about improving outcomes for people — and that makes him a good economist and a well-grounded leader," said Silvana Tenreyro, an economics professor at the London School of Economics who worked with Carney on the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee. "Mark was a stellar governor at the BOE, knowledgeable, hard-working and invariably driven by the Bank's mandate and mission."

Given his expertise in economics — a PhD from Oxford University is the least of his credentials — Carney would seem extremely well placed to outmaneuver Trump when it comes to maximizing prosperity for the nation. But history suggests that what works in economics doesn't always work in politics.

It is extremely rare for a central banker to go on to become a nation's political leader. When they have, they don't have a great record.

The most recent high-profile example is Mario Draghi, the former president of the European Central Bank who was invited to serve as Italy's prime minister when squabbling parties left the country's government in gridlock during the Covid-19 pandemic. He managed to push through an economic stimulus package, but he was forced to resign after less than two years.

Draghi never had to face voters in an election, and he was succeeded by Giorgia Meloni, a right-wing politician in the same vein as Trump. That suggests whatever technical expertise Draghi brought to the job wasn't particularly appreciated by the citizenry.

Another example is Manmohan Singh, India's prime minister from 2004 to 2014. He was praised for overseeing critical economic reforms as India integrated into world markets. He was also — unlike Mario Draghi — able to win elections. At the same time, with his quiet and composed demeanor, he was criticized in his second term for being indecisive as political scandals among his ministers arose, and his approval rating sank in his second term. Singh died at age 92 in December.

Central bankers are known for making difficult decisions, and raising interest rates to contain inflation is often unpopular. Yet there are certainly areas where politicians and monetary policymakers have overlapping skill sets — both have to build consensus and coalitions, and be the public face of national institutions.

Carney was arguably the political leader of the U.K. in 2016 — at least temporarily. In the hours after Britain voted to leave the European Union, Prime Minister David Cameron resigned, creating a political vacuum. The pound tanked. The nation looked to Carney for guidance, and he was able to calm nerves by saying the BOE would protect the financial system.

Carney's other claim to fame is navigating Canada, as BOC governor, through the 2008-09 financial crisis without falling into recession. It was the only G-7 country to manage that.

The biggest challenge might be selling his economic know-how to the people, as even those with the greatest knowledge don't always make the best connection with voters. As former European Commission President Jean-Claude Juncker once said: "We all know what to do, but we don't know how to get re-elected once we have done it."

Another problem is that Carney still represents the old world order that many people feel has left them behind. Frustration with the textbook way of doing government policy is what pushed many U.S. voters to Trump and, arguably, away from Canada's Trudeau, who stepped aside after his own ministers said they lost confidence in his policies.

Carney hasn't yet faced a popular vote and his tenure as prime minister may not last long. A federal election must be called this year, and he's expected to do it quickly. A vote has to come by Oct. 20 at the latest. Opposition leader Pierre Poilievre leads in opinion polls, but not by much. Carney has narrowed the gap to 1 point in opinion polls from almost 20 points a month ago.

So, despite central bankers' patchy record in politics, it looks like a lot of Canadians will be willing to take a chance on one in the face of a new economic crisis.

Write to Brian Swint at brian.swint@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: TSX Trims Weekly Losses
data.description:
The S&P/TSX Composite Index rose 0.5% to above the 24,300 level on Friday, trimming some of the weekly losses of over 1.5% and rebounding from four-month lows as positive economic data and the prospect of a policy turnaround drive optimism.

Manufacturing sales increased by 3% year-on-year in January and wholesale sales rose by 2.8% over the same period, signaling economic resilience amid persistent U.S. trade tensions.

Furthermore, the forthcoming swearing-in of former central banker Mark Carney as prime minister, who is expected to counteract U.S. tariff threats on Canadian goods, has added further support.

The recovery was led by tech and financial heavyweights, with e-commerce leader Shopify adding nearly 3% to recover from sharp losses, while Constellation Software and Celestica contributed additional gains of over 1% and 3% respectively.

Additionally, major banks such as RBC, TD Bank, and BMO each advanced more than 0.7%.

data.title: Travis Perkins Shares Fall on 2024 Earnings Delay
data.description:
(13:29 GMT) *Travis Perkins Backs 2024 View
(13:46 GMT) Travis Perkins Shares Fall on 2024 Earnings Delay

By Ian Walker

Travis Perkins shares fell after the company said that its 2024 earnings would be delayed as its auditor needs more time to complete standard procedures, and backed its guidance.

Shares were down 59.50 pence, or 10%, at 512.0 pence in early afternoon European trading. They are currently down 30% over the year to date.

The FTSE 250-listed builders' merchant and home improvement retailer group said Friday that it would publish earnings for the year ended Dec. 31 as soon as possible. They were originally due to be reported on March 18.

Travis Perkins said it expects adjusted operating profit--which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--for the year to be in line with guidance provided on Oct. 24. At that time the company cut its guidance for the metric to 135 million pounds ($174.8 million), from 150 million pounds previously forecast, which it blamed on a shortfall in the merchanting segment

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1330 GMT on Friday:

** Liberty Global < LBTYA.O > has approached Britain's Vodafone < VOD.L > for a possible acquisition of its stake in their Dutch joint venture VodafoneZiggo, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

** Recycled metal products maker Radius Recycling RDUS.O said on Thursday it had entered into a definitive merger agreement with Toyota Tsusho America, a U.S. subsidiary of Japanese trading house Toyota Tsusho Corp (TTC) 8015.T.

** Home brokerage Compass COMP.N is in advanced talks to acquire Warren Buffett's real-estate brokerage business, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: AeroVironment Says Army Cancels February Stop-Work Order
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

AeroVironment said Friday that the U.S. defense department has lifted the stop-work order issued last month.

The Department of the Army in February issued a stop-work order on certain existing U.S. government contracts, which included foreign military sales funded by the U.S. government by way of foreign military financing.

AeroVironment, a defense contractor, said that it is assessing the effect of the delay caused by the issuance of the stop-work order and the timing of its fulfilment of its obligations under the applicable contracts.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Sterling Receives Comptroller of the Currency Approval for Sale of Sterling Bank to EverBank
data.description:
Sterling Bancorp said Friday it has received approval from the Comptroller of the Currency to sell all of the issued and outstanding shares of its Sterling Bank and Trust unit to EverBank Financial.

Sterling said it will receive a fixed purchase price of $261 million in cash in the sale and that it intends to complete the transaction following the expiration of all applicable waiting periods.

The deal was first announced in September.

data.title: DEADLINE ALERT: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Investigates Claims on Behalf of Investors of Integral Ad Science
data.description:
Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson Encourages Investors Who Suffered Losses Exceeding $100,000 In Integral Ad Science To Contact Him Directly To Discuss Their Options

If you suffered losses exceeding $100,000 in Integral Ad Science between March 2, 2023 and February 27, 2024 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

[You may also click here for additional information]

New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - March 14, 2025) - Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP, a leading national securities law firm, is investigating potential claims against Integral Ad Science Holding Corp. ("Integral Ad Science" or the "Company") and reminds investors of the March 31, 2025 deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action that has been filed against the Company.

Faruqi & Faruqi is a leading national securities law firm with offices in New York, Pennsylvania, California and Georgia. The firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors since its founding in 1995. See www.faruqilaw.com.

As detailed below, the complaint alleges that the Company and its executives violated federal securities laws by making false and/or misleading statements and/or failing to disclose that: (1) that IAS was experiencing a new material trend of increased competitive pricing pressures and that, as a result, IAS had been forced to cut prices to compensate for weakening demand and slowing revenue growth; (2) that IAS's pricing function was no longer "favorable" and IAS could not sustain its pricing and drive price increases; (3) that pricing had become a key differentiator between IAS and its competitor necessary to close major renewals and new deals; and (4) as a result, the IAS's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

On February 27, 2024, after the market closed, IAS reported fourth quarter results for 2023 and announced lackluster revenue guidance below analysts' estimates. On an earnings call that same day, the Company admitted that these disappointing revenue numbers resulted from pricing cuts issued to customers across the Company's measurement and optimization businesses. Chief Executive Officer ("CEO") Lisa Utzschneider ("Utzschneider") stated, "[w]e are seeing more competitive pricing in measurement on a select group of large contract renewals in exchange for increased volume commitments and multi-year exclusive agreements."

In response to this news, on February 28, 2024, the Company's stock price declined $7.09 (or approximately 41%) to $10.01 per share from the previous day's close of $17.10 per share.

The court-appointed lead plaintiff is the investor with the largest financial interest in the relief sought by the class who is adequate and typical of class members who directs and oversees the litigation on behalf of the putative class. Any member of the putative class may move the Court to serve as lead plaintiff through counsel of their choice, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision to serve as a lead plaintiff or not.

Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP also encourages anyone with information regarding Integral Ad Science's conduct to contact the firm, including whistleblowers, former employees, shareholders and others.

To learn more about the Integral Ad Science class action, go to www.faruqilaw.com/IAS or call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, on X, or on Facebook.

Attorney Advertising. The law firm responsible for this advertisement is Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP (www.faruqilaw.com). Prior results do not guarantee or predict a similar outcome with respect to any future matter. We welcome the opportunity to discuss your particular case. All communications will be treated in a confidential manner.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/244486

data.title: Bausch Health Brief: Taking Bausch + Lomb private with a Third-party Buyer was one of several options explored, But That Didn't Go Through In Feb.
Market Chatter: Citigroup, Apollo Offer Private Staple Financing Option for Boeing Unit Sale
data.description:
Citigroup and Apollo Global Management are offering a private staple financing option for prospective buyers of Boeing's Jeppesen navigation unit, Bloomberg reported late Thursday, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The option would be the first private staple financing, which is a pre-arranged financing package offered to potential bidders for an acquisition, the report said.

The acquisition could be worth $7 billion, with debt financing ranging from $3 billion to $3.5 billion, the report said.

Talks are still ongoing, and the deal's terms and financing may change, the report said.

A Boeing representative told Bloomberg the company "does not comment on market rumors or speculation."

Citigroup and Apollo did not immediately reply to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)
